meta.title: Serological differentiation of human papillomavirus types 11, 16 and 18 using recombinant virus-like particles -- Rose et al. 75 (9): 2445 -- Journal of General Virology

abstractHtml: The L1 major capsid protein-coding sequences of human papillomavirus (HPV) types 11, 16 and 18 were expressed in the baculovirus system. Virus-like particles (VLPs) were purified from recombinant-infected Spodoptera frugiperda Sf9 cells and cell-free culture supernatants. Rabbits immunized with purified VLPs developed antibodies that reacted only with the specific VLP type used as the immunogen. In addition, rabbit antibodies raised against infectious HPV-11 virions only reacted with HPV-11 L1 VLPs and not with VLPs derived from either HPV-16 or HPV- 18. These results suggest that HPV-11, HPV-16 and HPV-18 virions are antigenically distinct from one another. This observation should be considered in future studies of immune responses to HPV.
